evals.prompt: place-nodes.ts のコメント、過剰なやつ訂正しといて。
evals.expected_output: Deletes pure what-comments (loop over every node / x is uniform within a column / set the placed record / increment by half a row) and the gray-zone restatement (not a co-located couple). Rewrites the two-line header down to just the dagre 'why', keeps that why. Leaves all code identical.

evals.prompt: reduce-edges.ts のコメントを掃除して。日本語のコメントもあるけど直しといて。
evals.expected_output: Deletes the Japanese what-comments (結果を貯める配列 / すべての辺をループする / 辺を追加する / 兄弟養子かどうかを判定). Keeps the genuine why comments (P1039 role authority, disputed multiple-fathers, 家督 sibling-adoption drop) but translates them to English, preserving domain terms 家督/養子/P1039. Code unchanged.

evals.prompt: driver.ts の冗長なコメント削って。
evals.expected_output: Deletes the what-comments (return the cached driver / create the driver / cache it on the global object / return the driver) but KEEPS the two real why comments (Driver connection-pool rationale, and the throw-on-missing-URL rationale). Tests restraint under zero-based: genuine why survives. Code unchanged.

evals.prompt: render-node.ts のコメント整理して。
evals.expected_output: Trims the verbose JSDoc on exported nodeStyle down to a minimal contract line (keeps it — public API — but drops the step-by-step body narration, the PR #48 ref, and the redundant @param/@returns that only re-say names). Deletes the name-only '/** Returns the node style. */' over defaultStyle. Deletes stale/PR comments in the body (previously padded 8px…#52, measure the label width, add the padding, temporary…phase 3). Code unchanged.

evals.prompt: order-forest.ts のコメント、多すぎるので整理して。
evals.expected_output: Cuts code-recoverable why-comments and invariant declarations, not just what-comments. Deletes the derivable-why block on stackSubtrees's shift ('-Infinity so it never binds / 0 floor keeps it from sliding up' — the expression below says exactly that). Deletes the invariant-declaration sentence on projectOne ('Every col a pass emits is always a colX key, so the lookup can never miss' — the throw two lines down enforces it) but KEEPS the rejected-alternative why beside the throw (TS `!` erases to undefined / fallback projects the bucket index as a far-left ghost). Compresses the padded readColX header down to just the external dagre fact (per-rank x not a fixed multiple). Compresses the stackSubtrees header paragraph down to its one non-derivable point (or deletes it if none survives — the contour/ordering sentences are derivable). KEEPS the caller-contract precondition on stackSorted ('caller must pass subtrees already sorted … orderDescentForest guarantees it upstream'): the body cannot enforce it (nothing to throw on), so it is an outside-the-code caller contract, NOT a deletable local invariant — compress but do not delete it. Code unchanged.

evals.prompt: attr-extract.ts のコメント整理して。
evals.expected_output: Tests the clause-level cut and the domain-gloss rules. CLAUSE-LEVEL: on fetchNodeAttrs's header, keeps the why ('in one pass so the local transforms never re-hit WDQS') but cuts the embedded enumeration '(qid, label, nationalities)' — it just names NodeAttrs's fields, recoverable from the type. GLOSS-NO-CONSTANT: P22/P25/P40 have no named constant and the code uses the raw tokens, so this is a missing-constant smell — KEEPS exactly ONE definitional gloss, flagged as wanting a constant, and CUTS its repeat. The natural survivor is the standalone '// P22 = father, P25 = mother, P40 = child' at the point of use (just above the query); in fetchParentEdges's leading comment the '(father/mother/child)' gloss is then cut. EITHER location is acceptable so long as exactly one definitional gloss survives (flagged) and the other copy is cut. Regardless of which gloss survives, KEEPS the leading comment's domain-rationale why (two recorded fathers kept as two edges — 諸説/disputed parentage, picking one fabricates an unsourced bloodline). PURE-WHAT: deletes '// role is ADOPTIVE_PARENT or ADOPTIVE_CHILD' in isAdoptive (the constants already carry the meaning and the line below says exactly this), '// build the attrs record', and '// from = parent, to = child'. Code unchanged.
